reason: Adding the noexec option to the /dev/shm mount point is a standard STIG hardening measure that does not involve partitioning, bootloader, or FIPS changes. It is a simple configuration edit that is unlikely to cause loss of access or critical service failure, though some applications that execute from /dev/shm may need to be verified.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; potential application incompatibility if software expects executable memory in /dev/shm
